findings.title: DynamoDB 400KB item limit blocks large galleries
findings.description: Gallery records exceed 400KB limit at 1500+ photos due to unbounded photo metadata storage.
findings.fix_suggestion: Migrate photos to separate DynamoDB table with gallery_id+filename composite key. Update get_gallery() and list_galleries() to join.

findings.title: Missing upload cancellation (AbortController)
findings.description: Photo uploads lack cancellation. Users on slow connections can't stop in-progress uploads.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add AbortController to upload fetch calls. Create cancel button in progress UI. Handle abort errors with retry.

findings.title: Test coverage gaps: auth handlers (0% coverage)
findings.description: Four Lambda handlers untested: auth_view, auth_download, maintenance, demo (~1200 LOC). Security-critical functions lack safety net.
findings.fix_suggestion: Write unit tests for auth (PIN validation, rate limiting, JWT, session expiry). Test maintenance (trash cleanup, orphans). Add 80% coverage gate to CI.

findings.title: Admin Lambda missing IAM permission for secret
findings.description: Lambda cannot fetch admin-api-key secret (iam.tf:227). Runtime errors when signing requests.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add IAM policy: secretsmanager:GetSecretValue for admin-api-key secret ARN. Test with real operations.

findings.title: WAF Web ACL not associated with resources
findings.description: WAF rules deployed but not attached to CloudFront or API Gateway. Inactive.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add WebACLAssociation resources to attach WAF ACL to CloudFront distributions. Test blocks with curl.

findings.title: Parallel uploads not implemented (sequential only)
findings.description: Photo uploads process one-at-a-time. 50-photo wedding takes N times longer than necessary.
findings.fix_suggestion: Replace sequential with Promise.all() for 4-6 concurrent. Implement queue and aggregate progress. Add concurrency limit.

findings.title: No single-photo download button in gallery view
findings.description: Clients can only download full gallery as ZIP. No quick 'Download this photo' button in lightbox.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add download icon to lightbox header. Expose presigned URL or simple download endpoint. Cache URLs 24h.

findings.title: Analogify Zen product has no shipped implementation
findings.description: Zen positioned as consumer product but exists only as marketing landing page. No backend, app, or downloads.
findings.fix_suggestion: Decision: (1) Ship Zen desktop/web app, (2) Remove from marketing, (3) Build as Studio module. For now route Zen prospects to Studio.

findings.title: 50+ API routes not synced to Terraform
findings.description: API Gateway routes defined in deploy.sh but not Terraform. Creates IaC drift.
findings.fix_suggestion: Extract all routes from deploy.sh into terraform/api_gateway.tf. Use modules for route groups. Verify match. Delete script definitions.

findings.title: 4 DynamoDB tables unmanaged by Terraform
findings.description: demo-sessions, sub-admins, comments, bans created manually. Not in Terraform. Drift-prone.
findings.fix_suggestion: Define all 4 tables in terraform/dynamodb.tf with billing, TTL, GSI. Add backup vault. Sync schema from running tables. Test apply.

findings.title: Missing Content Security Policy (CSP) headers
findings.description: CloudFront doesn't inject CSP. Only nosniff header set. No protection against XSS.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create ResponseHeadersPolicy with CSP: 'default-src self; script-src self; style-src self fonts.googleapis.com'. Apply to distributions.

findings.title: Invalid IAM actions and hardcoded account IDs
findings.description: s3:CopyObject (invalid), hardcoded account IDs in ARNs instead of data source.
findings.fix_suggestion: Replace s3:CopyObject with GetObject,PutObject. Replace hardcoded IDs with '${data.aws_caller_identity.current.account_id}'. Run plan.

findings.title: Mobile lightbox lacks swipe gestures
findings.description: Mobile lightbox doesn't support swipe navigation. Touch users must use buttons.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add touchstart/touchend listeners. Track swipe distance/velocity. Navigate on >50px swipe.

findings.title: Gallery wizard needs better error messages
findings.description: Wizard (new) lacks specific error messages for upload failures, processing errors.
findings.fix_suggestion: Parse error responses. Show specific messages: 'File too large (max 500MB)'. Handle rate limits, auth, file types separately.

findings.title: Access log needs pagination/filtering
findings.description: Unbounded access log table. Popular galleries have massive unfiltered lists.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add date range picker, action filter (view/download/comment/ban). Paginate 50/page. Sort by date/email.

findings.title: Photo ordering/drag-and-drop not implemented
findings.description: Photos appear in upload order. No reorder option for narrative flow.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add drag handles. Implement PATCH /photo-order endpoint. Persist in gallery.photos array.

findings.title: Missing loading states in critical flows
findings.description: Approve, trash, reprocess operations lack loading spinners.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add loading spinner component. Show during operations. Display duration estimate if >2s.

findings.title: Client favorites feature missing
findings.description: Clients can browse/download but can't save favorites. Useful signal for photographers.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add favorites table. Show heart icon in lightbox. POST /favorites. Display count in admin.

findings.title: No automated dependency scanning
findings.description: pyproject.toml pins versions but no security audit or patch management.
findings.fix_suggestion: Run pip-audit. Add GitHub Actions scan job. Consider dependabot.

findings.title: Hardcoded environment values in code
findings.description: Lambda handlers have hardcoded defaults for region, table/bucket names.
findings.fix_suggestion: Remove all defaults. Require all config in Lambda env vars. Add startup validation.

findings.title: API endpoints missing JSDoc/OpenAPI docs
findings.description: 131 functions in admin/handler.py lack docstrings. No OpenAPI spec.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add JSDoc with types, response shape, errors. Generate OpenAPI spec. Expose via Swagger UI.

findings.title: Architecture decision records (ADRs) missing
findings.description: Key decisions (serverless, DynamoDB, multi-style) documented only in commits/comments.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create docs/adr/. Write ADRs for serverless, DynamoDB, multi-style generation, photo table migration.

findings.title: Placeholder email addresses in code
findings.description: Support emails hardcoded as cody@codyjo.com. HANDOFF lists as pending.
findings.fix_suggestion: Move to env vars. Set to support@analogifystudio.com, noreply@analogifystudio.com. Test send.

findings.title: No public API/webhooks for integrations
findings.description: No way for agencies/tools to programmatically manage galleries. MODULARIZATION-PLAN mentions 'marketplace' as future.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement public API: (1) API key generation, (2) token auth on /api/* routes, (3) Webhooks. Start with gallery CRUD.

findings.title: No analytics dashboard for photographers
findings.description: Cost dashboard exists. No engagement analytics. Photographers don't see view trends or download patterns.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add analytics: views/downloads over time, top photos, client heatmap, geography. Use access_log data.

findings.title: Watermark feature incomplete or missing
findings.description: Admin.html has watermark UI but feature may not be implemented. MODULARIZATION-PLAN lists as Module 3 (future).
findings.fix_suggestion: Complete watermark in optimize pipeline or hide UI and implement as future module. Use feature flag.

findings.title: Preset/LUT import system for future Studio module
findings.description: ChromaHaus has 5 styles. No custom LUT/preset import. MODULARIZATION-PLAN Module 2 describes this.
findings.fix_suggestion: Plan: (1) preset table, (2) LUT loading from S3, (3) upload UI. Defer to Studio module.

findings.title: No Lambda artifact versioning strategy
findings.description: Admin Lambda uses built-artifact but no semantic versioning or rollback documented.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add git tag to artifact. Track version in Terraform. Add S3 versioning. Document rollback.

findings.title: No automated backup policy for DynamoDB
findings.description: Critical tables have no automated backups. Manual recovery would lose data.
findings.fix_suggestion: Enable PITR on critical tables. Add AWS Backup vault with 30d retention. Document recovery.

findings.title: Consider browser-based A11y (axe) tests for CI
findings.description: Manual accessibility reviews exist. No automated axe tests in CI. HANDOFF suggests if deeper coverage needed.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add playwright/cypress test with @axe-core/playwright. Run on key routes. Flag warnings on PR.

findings.title: No privacy audit department or findings aggregation
findings.description: The product supports 6 departments (QA, SEO, ADA, Compliance, Monetization, Product) but has no Privacy/Data Protection audit despite privacy being a critical compliance domain. The code comments reference a 'privacy' department and privacy-findings.json in some places, but there is no agent script, prompt, or dashboard for Privacy audits. Privacy findings can be created manually via task queue but not systematically audited.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create a complete Privacy/Data Protection department: (1) Write agents/prompts/privacy-audit.md with GDPR (right to erasure, data portability, consent), UK Online Safety Act (age verification, duty of care), and CCPA coverage. (2) Create agents/privacy-audit.sh script following existing patterns. (3) Create dashboard/privacy.html dashboard. (4) Update Makefile with privacy target. (5) Register privacy in workflow.py DEPARTMENT_SCRIPTS, aggregate.py, and HQ dashboard.

findings.title: No public API or webhook system for dashboard integration
findings.description: The Back Office is a closed system: findings are written to JSON files and synced to S3, but there is no documented public API for third-party integrations. Users cannot programmatically trigger audits, retrieve findings, subscribe to updates, or integrate findings into external SIEM/monitoring systems. The api_server.py and server.py exist but are local-only development tools, not production-grade APIs with authentication, rate limiting, or documentation.
findings.fix_suggestion: Design and document a public REST API: (1) Define endpoints: GET /api/v1/targets, GET /api/v1/targets/{name}/findings/{department}, POST /api/v1/targets/{name}/audit, GET /api/v1/audit-status/{job_id}. (2) Implement OAuth2 or API key authentication with per-target scoping. (3) Add rate limiting (per key/IP). (4) Document with OpenAPI 3.0 spec. (5) Create webhook system for audit completion (POST to user-provided URL). (6) Add client SDKs (Python, JS). This enables integrations and extends market reach.

findings.title: No real-time audit progress updates in dashboard
findings.description: The job progress dashboard (jobs.html) polls local-audit-log.json every 5 seconds to show agent progress. There are no WebSocket updates, no server-sent events (SSE), and no real-time UI refresh. If an audit takes 45 minutes (common for large repos), the user sees a static 5-second-stale status. The dashboard must refresh manually to see new findings.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement real-time job progress updates: (1) Add WebSocket support to server.py (use websockets or socket.io library). (2) Have agent scripts write progress to a job status server instead of files. (3) Server broadcasts updates to connected dashboard clients. (4) Update jobs.html to connect to WebSocket and render live findings as they arrive. (5) Add SSE fallback for environments where WebSocket isn't supported. This enables dramatic UX improvements.

findings.title: Dashboard filtering and search is limited
findings.description: The QA, SEO, and Product dashboards show findings but have limited filtering. Users can filter by repo and severity, but cannot filter by category, effort, or priority_phase. There is no full-text search across finding titles and descriptions. Finding tables with 100+ items require scrolling and manual inspection to find specific issues.
findings.fix_suggestion: Enhance dashboard filtering: (1) Add filter inputs for severity, category, effort, priority_phase. (2) Add full-text search box that filters findings by title + description in real-time. (3) Add 'quick filters' for common views (e.g., 'Must-Have', 'Next Sprint', 'High Effort'). (4) Persist filter state in URL query params (deeplinks). (5) Add column sorting. This is a low-effort high-value UX improvement.

findings.title: Configuration management is fragmented across multiple formats
findings.description: The project uses three config formats: YAML (config/backoffice.yaml), shell env (scripts/agent-runner.env), and Python dataclasses (backoffice/config.py). Documentation refers to config/targets.yaml, but the new code uses config/backoffice.yaml. Migration from old to new format is incomplete — some shell scripts still reference the old config path.
findings.fix_suggestion: Complete the migration to unified config: (1) Consolidate all config into a single config/backoffice.yaml schema (already exists in config.py). (2) Update all shell scripts to use the new path. (3) Add a config migration tool that converts old format to new. (4) Remove references to legacy format from README. (5) Add config validation at startup (fail loudly if schema is invalid). (6) Update workflow.py to use Config object instead of direct YAML loads.

findings.title: No request tracing or distributed tracing for multi-department audits
findings.description: When users run audit-all (6 departments in sequence or parallel), there is no way to trace a single audit through all 6 agents. Logs are written separately, job status is per-department, and findings have no shared trace ID. If an audit fails midway (e.g., SEO completes, but ADA hangs), it's hard to correlate what happened.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add request tracing: (1) Generate a trace_id (UUID) at audit start. (2) Pass trace_id to all agent scripts via environment variable. (3) Include trace_id in all log output (structured logs). (4) Add trace_id to findings JSON. (5) Add trace_id to job status records. (6) Create a dashboard view that shows audit timeline by department with trace_id linking all artifacts.

findings.title: No audit history or time-series comparison
findings.description: Each audit overwrites the previous results. There is no audit history — users cannot see how many findings existed last week, which findings were fixed, or track improvement over time. The regression module tracks test results but not audit findings.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add audit history: (1) Archive findings to results/history/{repo}/{dept}/{timestamp}/findings.json on each audit. (2) Create comparison endpoint: GET /api/v1/targets/{name}/findings/{dept}?compare_to=<timestamp> returns delta (added, fixed, unchanged). (3) Add 'Improvements' chart to dashboard showing critical findings over time. (4) Add ability to export finding timeseries data for analysis. (5) Consider S3 versioning for long-term storage.

findings.title: Task queue UI is sparse — no visual task timeline or burndown
findings.description: The task queue dashboard (tasks.html) shows a list of tasks but no visual representation of progress. There is no burndown chart, kanban board, or sprint view. Users manually track which tasks are done vs. in-progress by scrolling a list.
findings.fix_suggestion: Enhance task queue UI: (1) Add kanban board view (Proposed → Ready → In Progress → Blocked → Review → Done). (2) Add sprint view with burndown chart. (3) Add task search and filtering. (4) Make tasks draggable between columns (statuses). (5) Add task detail modal with notes and history. Medium effort, high UX impact.

findings.title: No error recovery for failed agent scans
findings.description: If an agent script fails (e.g., agent runner process crashes, network timeout, out of memory), the audit stops and users must manually restart. There is no retry logic, no partial result recovery, or checkpoint mechanism. If audit-all starts 6 departments but department 3 fails, all subsequent departments are skipped.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add retry and checkpoint logic: (1) Implement exponential backoff retry (3 attempts) in agent runner. (2) Add checkpoint mechanism — save partial results after each department completes. (3) Add resume-audit command that skips completed departments and retries failed ones. (4) Track retry count in job status. (5) Add circuit breaker to cancel remaining departments if 2+ departments fail.

findings.title: S3 sync is synchronous and blocks dashboard deployment
findings.description: The sync command uploads findings and dashboards to S3 synchronously. If S3 is slow or network is congested, the entire audit workflow blocks. Users waiting for results must wait for S3 upload to complete. No progress indication during upload.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement async S3 sync: (1) Move S3 uploads to a background thread pool. (2) Return upload status immediately after initiating transfers. (3) Add upload progress tracking (bytes transferred / total). (4) Queue pending uploads so multiple audits don't overwhelm network. (5) Add dashboard endpoint to check sync status. (6) Consider using multipart upload for large files.

findings.title: No rate limiting or quota enforcement in API server
findings.description: The api_server.py accepts scan requests with no rate limiting, quota enforcement, or cost tracking. A malicious actor could trigger unlimited audits, exhausting resources (compute, AI tokens, S3 bandwidth). No per-user/per-target quotas.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add rate limiting and quotas: (1) Implement per-API-key rate limiting (token bucket, e.g., 10 audits/hour). (2) Track quota consumption per target per month. (3) Reject requests exceeding quota. (4) Add cost tracking (estimate AI tokens per department, charge appropriately). (5) Add admin dashboard to set quotas per customer. (6) Add alerts for quota approaching or overages.

findings.title: Hardcoded department names scattered across codebase
findings.description: Department names ('qa', 'seo', 'ada', etc.) are hardcoded as strings in multiple places: DEPARTMENT_SCRIPTS dicts, Makefile targets, dashboard file names, prompt directories. Adding a new department requires changes in 8+ locations with high risk of inconsistency.
findings.fix_suggestion: Centralize department registry: (1) Create backoffice/departments.py with a DEPARTMENTS constant (dataclass or registry). (2) Each department: name, script_path, prompt_path, dashboard_path, findings_filename, dashboard_json_field. (3) Import DEPARTMENTS everywhere instead of hardcoding. (4) Generate Makefile targets from registry. (5) Validate at startup that all department files exist. (6) Add tooling to scaffold a new department (auto-generate files from registry).

findings.title: No API documentation or client SDKs
findings.description: While the Python package is documented in README, there is no formal API specification for the server.py or api_server.py endpoints. No OpenAPI/Swagger spec. No client library examples for other languages. Users integrating with Back Office must reverse-engineer the JSON format.
findings.fix_suggestion: Document the API: (1) Write openapi.yaml spec for all endpoints (GET /api/v1/findings, POST /api/v1/audit, etc.). (2) Generate API docs with Swagger UI. (3) Add client library generators (OpenAPI codegen for Python, JS, Go). (4) Add curl/requests examples for common workflows. (5) Publish API docs to a dedicated page or README section. (6) Version the API (v1, v2) to enable backward-compatibility.

findings.title: No audit scheduling or cron integration
findings.description: Audits are always manual — users must run `make product TARGET=/path` or click 'Run Scan' in the dashboard. There is no way to schedule audits to run nightly, weekly, or on a cron schedule. The TODO.md mentions 'nightly automated QA scan cron job' as a future feature.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add audit scheduling: (1) Add schedule config to backoffice.yaml (e.g., audit_schedule: 'nightly', 'weekly', or cron expression). (2) Implement scheduler in backoffice/scheduler.py using APScheduler. (3) Run scheduler as background service (or cron via systemd timer). (4) Add ability to manually trigger audit-on-demand. (5) Store last audit timestamp in job status. (6) Show 'Last audited: X hours ago' on dashboard with warning if stale (>24h).

findings.title: No email notifications for audit completion or new critical findings
findings.description: Audits complete silently. There is no email notification when an audit finishes, no alerts for new critical findings, and no digest emails. Teams must manually check the dashboard to see if anything has changed.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add notifications: (1) Integrate SES for email delivery. (2) Send email on audit completion with summary (total findings, critical count, trend). (3) Add email alerts for new critical findings. (4) Support digest emails (daily/weekly summary). (5) Add Slack integration (optional) for critical findings. (6) Allow users to subscribe/unsubscribe per target and severity. (7) Include direct links to findings in emails.

findings.title: Monolithic agent runner script duplicates logic across departments
findings.description: Each department has its own audit script (qa-scan.sh, seo-audit.sh, etc.) which mostly duplicate the same logic: load config, build prompt, call agent runner, write findings to JSON. The scripts have >70% overlap with minor variations (prompt file path, findings filename).
findings.fix_suggestion: Refactor agent scripts: (1) Create a generic agent-runner wrapper script that accepts department name and prompt file. (2) Have qa-scan.sh, seo-audit.sh, etc. call the wrapper with department-specific args. (3) Or: port all agent logic to Python (backoffice.audit_manager module) and call from a single CLI entry point. (4) Update Makefile to use the unified wrapper.

findings.title: Test coverage gaps in API server and dashboard server
findings.description: The test suite has excellent coverage (~70%+) but server.py and api_server.py are under-tested. test_servers.py covers basic routing and job submission but lacks tests for WebSocket behavior, concurrent job handling, and error scenarios (bad auth, malformed requests).
findings.fix_suggestion: Expand server tests: (1) Add tests for concurrent job submission (same target, different departments). (2) Mock WebSocket behavior and test lifecycle (connect → receive updates → disconnect). (3) Add tests for auth failures, malformed payloads, missing params. (4) Add integration test with actual agent script. (5) Aim for 85%+ coverage of server code.

findings.title: No TypeScript in dashboard — frontend is vanilla JS
findings.description: Dashboard HTML files (qa.html, product.html, etc.) use vanilla JavaScript with no type checking, build step, or module system. JavaScript is written inline in HTML <script> tags. As complexity grows, this becomes hard to maintain.
findings.fix_suggestion: Modernize dashboard frontend: (1) Extract JS from HTML into separate modules. (2) Add TypeScript for type safety. (3) Add a build step (esbuild or Vite) to bundle and minify. (4) Add ESLint for code quality. (5) Consider lightweight framework (Vue.js, Svelte, htmx) if complexity justifies it. Start with a single dashboard (qa.html) as pilot. Medium effort, long-term productivity gain.

findings.title: Dashboard styling uses inline CSS in HTML files
findings.description: Dashboard styling is defined inline in <style> blocks within each HTML file. Styles are duplicated across dashboards (colors, fonts, spacing). No CSS-in-JS, no Tailwind, no design system. Changing a color or font requires editing multiple files.
findings.fix_suggestion: Extract shared design system: (1) Create dashboard/styles/design-system.css with shared colors, typography, spacing, components. (2) Import into all dashboards. (3) Or: use Tailwind CSS with PostCSS. (4) Create a component library for common UI elements (finding cards, stat cards, buttons). (5) Document design system (colors, spacing, typography) in a style guide.

findings.title: Missing architecture decision records (ADRs)
findings.description: The codebase has undergone major migrations (shell scripts → Python package, multiple config formats). No ADRs document why certain architectural choices were made, trade-offs considered, or decisions that should not be revisited.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create architecture documentation: (1) Write docs/ADRs.md or docs/adr/ directory with ADR files. (2) Document key decisions: Python package structure, config format consolidation, S3/CloudFront vs. other deployment, agent architecture. (3) Include trade-offs (shell scripts vs. Python, sync vs. async). (4) Create ARCHITECTURE.md explaining system design, data flow, and layers. (5) Document API contract expectations.

findings.title: Opportunity: Self-hosted audit dashboard for target repos
findings.description: Currently, findings are synced to a central S3 bucket and viewed in a central Back Office dashboard. A growth opportunity is allowing teams to host a self-contained audit dashboard in their own repo. Imagine a /audit dashboard that shows findings for a single target repo (embedded or standalone).
findings.fix_suggestion: Create repo-specific audit dashboard: (1) Design a minimal dashboard that takes repo name/URL as input. (2) Generate a standalone HTML file that can be committed to /audit or /_audit in target repo. (3) Embed a JS script that fetches findings from Back Office API. (4) Or: generate a static dashboard with pre-computed findings (works offline). (5) Include CI step to regenerate dashboard after each audit. (6) Market this as 'Audit Transparency' feature.

findings.title: Opportunity: Benchmark feature — compare audits across portfolio
findings.description: Users cannot compare audits across different target repos (e.g., 'which repo has the most critical findings?', 'product readiness scores across portfolio'). Adding a benchmark/comparison dashboard would surface this data.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create portfolio benchmark dashboard: (1) Aggregate findings across all repos by department. (2) Create heatmap: repos × departments, cells show severity distribution. (3) Rank repos by readiness score, critical findings, etc. (4) Show trend lines (readiness over time across portfolio). (5) Identify outliers ('this repo has 10x the critical issues').

findings.title: Opportunity: Fix recommendations engine powered by findings
findings.description: Back Office produces findings but no AI-powered fix recommendations. A growth opportunity is integrating Claude to generate PRs or fix suggestions based on findings from each department (e.g., QA findings → suggested code fixes, SEO findings → suggested content changes).
findings.fix_suggestion: Add AI-powered fix recommendations: (1) After each department audit, feed findings to Claude with 'generate fix PR' prompt. (2) Generate fix branches with PRs for highest-priority findings. (3) Let users review and merge PRs. (4) Track fixes in task queue. (5) Integrate with GitHub API. (6) This requires careful prompt design to avoid breaking changes.

findings.title: Opportunity: Mobile app for viewing audit results
findings.description: Currently, audit dashboards are web-only and desktop-optimized. A mobile app (iOS/Android) would enable team members to check findings on-the-go, receive push notifications for critical issues, and triage findings from anywhere.
findings.fix_suggestion: Build mobile app: (1) Consider React Native or Flutter for code sharing. (2) Create apps for iOS and Android. (3) Connect to Back Office API. (4) Features: audit list, findings by severity, drill-down to details, push notifications. (5) Publish to App Store and Google Play. (6) This is a long-term growth play.

findings.title: Opportunity: Compliance certification badges
findings.description: Teams could earn 'ADA Certified', 'GDPR Compliant', 'Monetization Ready' badges by passing department audits with no critical findings. Badges could be embedded in repos (README badge) or displayed on team websites.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add compliance badges: (1) Define badge criteria per department (e.g., 'ADA Certified' = 0 critical ADA findings + 85+ compliance score). (2) Generate dynamic badge SVG that teams can embed in READMEs. (3) Track badge history. (4) Display badges on HQ dashboard. (5) Consider integration with Open Badges standard.

findings.title: Missing example configurations and sample dashboards
findings.description: README has one config example (backoffice.example.yaml). No example for multiple targets, no example for different department combinations, no explanation of common configuration patterns.
findings.fix_suggestion: Enhance docs: (1) Create config/examples/ directory with multiple example configurations. (2) Add configs for: single target, multi-target, QA-only, production-like setup. (3) Add commented-out optional fields. (4) Add 'Getting Started' walkthrough with config and first audit. (5) Add troubleshooting section for common config issues.

findings.title: No support for multi-branch or feature-branch audits
findings.description: Audits always target a single branch (usually main). There's no way to audit a feature branch, pull request, or ephemeral environment to get feedback before merge.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add branch and PR audit support: (1) Allow specifying branch in config or CLI: audit --branch feature/x. (2) Check out branch before audit. (3) Integrate with GitHub to comment audit results on PRs. (4) Add GitHub Actions workflow to run audits on every PR. (5) Gate PR merge on critical findings. This would position Back Office as part of CI.

findings.title: Agent timeouts are not configurable per department
findings.description: Agent scripts have hard-coded timeout behavior. If an agent takes longer than expected (large repo, slow AI API), it may timeout with no way to extend the timeout. Large audits like compliance can take 30+ minutes but users cannot adjust timeout.
findings.fix_suggestion: Make timeouts configurable: (1) Add timeout_seconds config per department in backoffice.yaml. (2) Pass timeout to agent scripts. (3) Set reasonable defaults (QA: 30min, SEO: 20min, compliance: 45min). (4) Document expected timeouts by repo size.

findings.title: Bookmarks can be managed but not created anywhere in the product UI
findings.description: The app advertises `/bookmarks` as a core surface and the empty state tells users to look for a bookmark icon while reading, but production code never calls `saveBookmark()`. Users can edit or delete bookmarks only after data somehow already exists.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add bookmark actions to the daily verse, books reader, and study reader surfaces. Wire them to `saveBookmark()`, show saved-state feedback, prevent duplicate bookmarks per reference/version, and add tests covering bookmark creation from at least one reading flow.

findings.title: Account backup is write-only with no restore or cloud rehydration flow
findings.description: The frontend can push studies, journals, bookmarks, and daily history to the account API, but it never reads that account data back into the device. The backend already exposes authenticated GET routes for studies, journals, bookmarks, and daily verses, yet the product offers only export/import or manual backup from local storage.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add an account restore/sync flow in Settings and optionally at sign-in. Fetch account datasets via the existing GET endpoints, decrypt journal payloads when needed, let the user choose merge vs replace, and surface conflict resolution rules for duplicates and newer local data.

findings.title: Email verification backend exists but users have no verification or resend UI
findings.description: Registration sends a verification code and the API supports both verify and resend actions, but the frontend only shows a passive status string. There is no screen, form, or CTA to enter a verification code or request a new one.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a verification card in Settings or after registration with code input, resend button, success state, and token/user refresh after verification. Reuse the existing backend routes and expose clear copy about why verification matters.

findings.title: Account deletion is implemented in the API but missing from the product
findings.description: The backend supports authenticated account deletion with password confirmation and full DynamoDB cleanup, but there is no corresponding UI in Settings or elsewhere.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a destructive account section in Settings with password confirmation, explicit warnings about local-vs-account data loss, success/logout handling, and a final confirmation dialog before calling `DELETE /api/account`.

findings.title: Study history saves scaffolding prompts instead of the actual AI study output
findings.description: When a study session is saved, the app stores the generated prompt list as `commentary` before the user interacts with AI chat. Actual assistant replies and follow-up discussion are not persisted back into the study record.
findings.fix_suggestion: Persist the initial AI commentary and subsequent assistant/user messages to the saved study session. Either store the transcript or a summary plus the latest commentary, and update the history UI to render that richer session data.

findings.title: AI failure fallback tells users the live study feature is still coming soon
findings.description: If `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is missing or the interpret request fails, the study chat silently falls back to hardcoded responses. Some of those responses explicitly say the full AI study is coming soon even though the app is marketed as already having AI study.
findings.fix_suggestion: Replace the canned 'coming soon' copy with explicit degraded-mode messaging. Distinguish configuration errors, signed-out states, and transient API failures, and offer retry or sign-in guidance without pretending the feature is unfinished.

findings.title: PWA offline support covers only a subset of the app's main surfaces
findings.description: The service worker precaches the home page plus Daily, Study, Plans, Books, and Journal, but excludes several first-class routes such as History, Insights, Bookmarks, Reflections, Settings, Updates, and auth pages.
findings.fix_suggestion: Expand the precache list to include all static local-first routes, validate offline navigation for installed PWAs, and add fallback messaging for surfaces that still require network resources such as AI study or remote Bible text fetches.

findings.title: Terraform provisions placeholder Lambda artifacts instead of deployable application code
findings.description: Both Lambda resources point at `placeholder.zip`, so infrastructure apply alone cannot produce a working backend. That makes disaster recovery, new-environment bootstrapping, and infra verification dependent on a separate deployment path.
findings.fix_suggestion: Document and enforce the two-step deploy path, or better, package real Lambda artifacts in CI and feed versioned build outputs into Terraform so infra can reference deployable code instead of placeholders.

findings.title: Existing insights data could drive personalized nudges and weekly review features
findings.description: The app already computes top books, study cadence, mood distribution, awaiting-reflection counts, prayer-request totals, and plan summaries. That data currently stops at passive display instead of powering personalized loops.
findings.fix_suggestion: Prototype one growth loop first: a weekly review card in Insights or Home that surfaces unfinished reflections, your most-studied book, and a next-step suggestion. Measure return visits before expanding into reminders or recommendation systems.

findings.title: Profile name editing is available in the API but absent from Settings
findings.description: The backend supports changing a user's display name and issuing a refreshed token, but Settings only renders the current name and email. Users cannot correct or personalize their profile after registration.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add inline name editing in Settings with validation, submit/cancel states, and token/user refresh using the existing `PUT /api/auth/name` endpoint.

findings.title: No email newsletter or mailing list integration
findings.description: The site lacks any email list signup mechanism or newsletter integration. Visitors cannot opt-in to receive product updates, blog announcements, or insights about the studio's work. This is a critical gap for audience building and direct communication.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a newsletter signup form section on the homepage (or in footer). Integrate with an email service like Mailchimp, Substack, ConvertKit, or AWS SES Lambda. Include opt-in checkbox on contact form. Display signup prompt on blog pages. Add post-subscribe confirmation and welcome sequence.

findings.title: Product pages lack external CTAs to canonical product domains
findings.description: Product pages are bridge pages that describe products but provide no clear call-to-action links to actual product websites. Visitors learn about a product but cannot easily access it.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add prominent CTA button to each product page linking to the canonical domain. Example: 'Visit Analogify Studio' or 'Launch Analogify'. Include CTA near top of page, in footer of page, and in product card metadata.

findings.title: Limited blog content (only 2 published posts)
findings.description: The blog currently has only 2 published posts. For a portfolio and thought leadership site, this severely limits content visibility, SEO value, and audience engagement.
findings.fix_suggestion: Establish a content calendar and publish 2-4 posts per month. Topics: product strategy, privacy-first design principles, automation thinking, case studies, lessons learned. Repurpose existing handoff documents and learning notes into blog posts.

findings.title: No product comparison or feature matrix
findings.description: Visitors cannot easily compare the 5 products in the portfolio. Each product is described individually with no side-by-side view showing which products serve which use cases or audience segments.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create a new page /products/comparison or add a comparison section to the homepage. Build a comparison matrix showing: product name, category, use case, audience, key features, privacy posture. Allow filtering/sorting by category or feature.

findings.title: Contact form lacks prominent success state feedback
findings.description: The contact form has validation and feedback messaging, but the success message uses color change only. For critical user actions, this is subtle and may not grab attention.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a success modal, toast notification, or banner that appears after form submission. Include a checkmark icon and clear messaging: 'Your message was sent successfully! I'll respond within 48 hours.' Option: redirect to a thank-you page.

findings.title: Blog search/filter state not persisted
findings.description: The blog page has search, product, category, and tag filters, but the filter state is not persisted in URL parameters. After filtering, refreshing the page resets all filters.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement query parameter handling for filters (e.g., ?q=privacy&product=tnbm-tarot&category=reflection). Update URL on filter change and restore filters from URL on page load. Consider using a state library or URL API.

findings.title: No guest post or contributed content section
findings.description: The blog is author-only (Cody Jo Eflin). There's no mechanism for guest contributors, interviews, or contributed pieces. This limits content diversity and audience growth.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add 'author' field to blog content schema (src/content.config.ts). Create /contribute page with guidelines for guest posts. Plan quarterly interviews or guest articles from other privacy/automation thought leaders.

findings.title: No case studies or success stories published
findings.description: The portfolio is feature-focused but lacks real-world case studies showing how products solve user problems. This reduces credibility and social proof.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create case study templates and interview customers for 2-3 documented success stories per product. Publish on dedicated /case-studies page or as blog series. Include metrics: time saved, friction reduced, privacy gains. Add short testimonials to product pages.

findings.title: No back-to-top button for long pages
findings.description: Long pages (especially the homepage) lack a back-to-top button or keyboard shortcut. Desktop users scrolling far down have no quick return mechanism.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a floating 'Back to Top' button (fixed position, bottom-right) that appears after scrolling past the fold. Implement smooth scroll-to-top behavior. Alternatively, add keyboard shortcut (e.g., Home key) for back-to-top.

findings.title: Product pages don't show related/recommended products
findings.description: Each product page stands alone. There's no related products or also explore section that cross-references other products in the portfolio.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a Related Products section at the bottom of each product page. Show 2-3 other products from the portfolio, prioritizing complementary categories (e.g., on Cordivent, suggest Analogify). Link to those product pages.

findings.title: Limited test coverage (2 test files only)
findings.description: Test coverage is minimal: only 2 test files covering product data and build artifacts. No tests for components, pages, or user interactions.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add unit tests for: FadeIn component intersection observer, Nav dropdown behavior, blog filter logic, contact form validation. Add E2E tests (Playwright or Cypress) for: navigation links, form submission, responsive layout on mobile.

findings.title: Blog filtering logic lacks URL state management
findings.description: Blog filtering is implemented with inline JavaScript but doesn't leverage browser history or URL APIs for state persistence.
findings.fix_suggestion: Refactor filtering to use URL search parameters (e.g., ?q=search&product=slug&category=topic&tag=tag). Populate URL on filter change and restore filters from URL on load. Consider extracting to a separate utility function.

findings.title: Contact form backend lacks documented error handling
findings.description: Contact form calls AWS Lambda endpoint but error handling is generic. No documented API contract or failure scenarios.
findings.fix_suggestion: Document the Lambda API contract: expected request shape, validation rules, possible error responses. Add logging/monitoring for form submissions. Consider using a form service (Formspree, Basin, etc.) instead of custom Lambda.

findings.title: Inline styles and scripts could be modularized
findings.description: Homepage and blog pages include large <style> and <script> blocks inline. As the site grows, this pattern doesn't scale well.
findings.fix_suggestion: Extract shared styles to src/styles/ modules. Move reusable JS logic to separate utility files. Consider using Astro's CSS modules or a utility class system (Tailwind). Keep component-specific styles inline if small.

findings.title: No breadcrumb navigation on nested pages
findings.description: Nested pages (product pages, blog detail pages) don't show breadcrumb navigation. Users have no clear indication of page hierarchy.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add breadcrumb component to Base layout (or include in Nav). Show hierarchy: Home > Section > Current Page. For blog: Home > Blog > [Product] > Article. For products: Home > Projects > Product.

findings.title: No alternative contact methods beyond form
findings.description: The only way to contact Cody Jo is through the homepage contact form. No email address, phone number, or direct social media handle is provided for quick outreach.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add email contact option: display plaintext email (or use email obfuscation) in footer or contact section. Consider adding social media handles if Cody Jo is active there. Add link to LinkedIn profile.

findings.title: Form error messages could be more specific
findings.description: Contact form validation error messages are generic (e.g., 'Please fill in all required fields'). They don't specify which field is missing.
findings.fix_suggestion: Track which field failed validation. Display field-specific error messages or add visual highlighting (red border, error icon) on invalid fields. Example: show tooltip or inline error near the field.

findings.title: Blog post metadata missing reading time estimate
findings.description: Blog post cards show date, product, category, and tags, but no reading time estimate or word count.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add readingTime field to blog post frontmatter or calculate from markdown. Display '~5 min read' or '2000 words' on post cards. Add reading time to blog detail pages.

findings.title: No CI/CD pipeline configured
findings.description: The HANDOFF.md documents manual S3 sync and CloudFront invalidation steps. No automated CI/CD (GitHub Actions, etc.) to deploy on commit or PR merge.
findings.fix_suggestion: Set up GitHub Actions workflow to: run tests, build site, deploy to S3 on push to main. Add pull request checks to run npm run check and npm test. Document deployment process.

findings.title: No last updated timestamp on pages
findings.description: Blog posts and product pages don't show a last updated date, only the publish date. For evergreen content, this can make it seem stale.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add updated field to blog post frontmatter. Display both publish and update date on post pages (e.g., 'Published March 5 | Updated March 20'). Update on any edits.

findings.title: No HTML sitemap page
findings.description: While an XML sitemap is generated (/sitemap-index.xml), there's no user-friendly /sitemap page showing all site pages in a browsable format.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create src/pages/sitemap.astro page that lists all main pages and blog posts in a browsable format. Include Last Updated dates. Link from footer or help section.

findings.title: No FAQ or help/support page
findings.description: There's no FAQ, help page, or support documentation. New visitors may have questions about the products or how the studio works.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create /faq page with common questions: What are the differences between products? Do you offer consulting? How do I report a bug? How is my data handled? Link from footer, header, and product pages.

findings.title: No CONTRIBUTING.md or community guidelines
findings.description: While the repo has CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md, there's no CONTRIBUTING.md documenting how external contributors can help (if applicable).
findings.fix_suggestion: If community contributions aren't planned, no action needed. If they are: create CONTRIBUTING.md with guidelines for: reporting issues, submitting PRs, coding standards, review process. Link from README.

findings.title: Plausible analytics lacks event tracking
findings.description: The site uses Plausible analytics for page views but doesn't track user interactions (form submission, button clicks, etc.) with custom events.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add Plausible event tracking to: contact form submission, product page visits, blog filter interactions, navigation clicks. Use Plausible's JavaScript API (window.plausible) to log custom events.

findings.title: Product pages don't show status indicator
findings.description: Product pages (especially bridge pages) don't indicate whether a product is actively maintained, in maintenance mode, or archived.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add status field to ProductMeta: active, maintained, legacy, archived. Display status badge on product pages (e.g., Actively Developed or Maintenance Mode). Update src/data/products.ts and ProductPage component.

findings.title: No API documentation or integration guides
findings.description: If any of the products offer APIs, SDKs, or webhooks, there's no documentation on codyjo.com. No integration guides or developer resources.
findings.fix_suggestion: If products have APIs: create /developers page with API docs, SDK reference, quickstart guides, code examples. Consider using Swagger/OpenAPI for API documentation. If not applicable, disregard.

findings.title: No user testimonials or social proof section
findings.description: The portfolio lacks user testimonials, quotes, or success metrics that demonstrate product value and build trust.
findings.fix_suggestion: Collect testimonials from product users. Create a testimonials section on homepage with 3-5 short quotes + customer name/role. Add metrics to product pages (if available): number of active users, events managed, photos processed, etc.

findings.title: No light mode / dark mode toggle
findings.description: The site uses a dark aesthetic and doesn't offer a light mode option. Some users may prefer light backgrounds for readability.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add CSS media query: @media (prefers-color-scheme: light) { /* light colors */ }. Optionally add a toggle button to override system preference. Use localStorage to persist user choice.

findings.title: No real-time deployment status updates or webhooks
findings.description: Deployments show status as 'pending', 'running', 'complete', or 'failed', but the UI requires manual refresh to see updates. There is no polling mechanism, WebSocket connection, or webhook system to push status changes to the browser.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement auto-polling with exponential backoff: poll every 5 seconds initially, backing off to 30 seconds once deployment status is 'complete' or 'failed'. Show loading spinner and last-update timestamp on deployment rows. Alternatively, implement Server-Sent Events (SSE) from backend for true push updates.

findings.title: No deployment cloning or configuration templates
findings.description: After a successful deployment, users who want to create a similar environment must re-enter all parameters manually. There is no 'Clone Deployment' or 'Export/Import' workflow beyond the basic export-config button.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add 'Clone Deployment' button on the detail view that pre-fills the deploy form with the original deployment's parameters. Store deployment config in metadata when created so it can be reproduced exactly.

findings.title: No audit trail or activity history visible in UI
findings.description: Backend logs audit events (created, destroyed) to metadata.json with audit_trail array, but the UI does not expose this history. Users cannot see who created/destroyed a deployment, when, or from what IP.
findings.fix_suggestion: Render audit_trail in the deployment detail view after console outputs. Show action, timestamp, and user for each event. This requires no API changes, only UI changes.

findings.title: No success confirmation or notification after deployment creation
findings.description: When users click 'Deploy', they see a confirmation modal, then immediately switch to detail view showing 'pending' status. There is no success toast, confirmation message, or clear indication that the deployment was accepted.
findings.fix_suggestion: After successful deployment creation, show a success toast message (e.g., 'Deployment queued! Redirecting to details...'). Display the deployment ID prominently on the detail page.

findings.title: No search, filter, or sort on deployments list
findings.description: The deployments list shows all deployments in reverse chronological order with no way to filter by architecture, status, owner, date range, or search by name/project.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add filter/search row above the deployments table with: search box (name/project), status dropdown, architecture dropdown, date range picker. Wire to frontend filtering or backend query parameters.

findings.title: No bulk operations for destroying multiple deployments
findings.description: Users can only destroy one deployment at a time via the detail view. No checkbox selection on list view or bulk destroy action.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add checkboxes to deployments table. Add 'Destroy Selected' button that appears when rows are checked.

findings.title: No form reset or 'Clear All' button
findings.description: After filling out a complex deployment form, users cannot easily reset all fields to defaults. They must manually clear each field or refresh the page.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a 'Reset Form' button next to Deploy that clears all fields to defaults and regenerates the deployment name.

findings.title: Minimal error context and no ability to retry failed deployments
findings.description: If Cloud Build fails, users see a 'failed' status and can only read the Cloud Build log URL. They cannot view error summary in UI or re-submit with same config.
findings.fix_suggestion: Extract last 20 lines of Cloud Build log and display in detail view. Add 'Retry Deployment' button for failed deployments that re-submits with original config.

findings.title: TTL (time-to-live) is advisory only, not auto-enforced
findings.description: Users can set a TTL in hours but the backend does not automatically destroy expired deployments. Only a list_expired() endpoint exists.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement a background job (Cloud Scheduler + Cloud Function) that calls list_expired() and destroys each. Show warning in UI when within 1 hour of expiry.

findings.title: No console access instructions visible in UI
findings.description: After deployment succeeds, the detail view does not show how to access the PE console. Users must infer from raw outputs or read the README.
findings.fix_suggestion: Format console_url as a prominent button/link with instructions. Display username 'admin'. Provide copy-to-clipboard for SSH commands.

findings.title: Impact Analysis addon doesn't affect actual sizing recommendations
findings.description: The 'Impact Analysis' checkbox is in the form but doesn't propagate to backend or affect actual sizing deployed.
findings.fix_suggestion: Wire Impact Analysis through to backend or remove local adjustment if it doesn't affect actual deployment.

findings.title: No integration with GCP to show real-time cost tracking
findings.description: UI shows pre-deployment cost estimates but doesn't track actual spend. Estimates use hardcoded rates without regional variance.
findings.fix_suggestion: Integrate with GCP Cloud Billing API for regional pricing. Display actual billed spend after deployment.

findings.title: Long form lacks progressive disclosure and inline help
findings.description: The deployment form has 8 fieldsets with 20+ fields. New users are overwhelmed by PE architecture knowledge requirements.
findings.fix_suggestion: Break into multi-step wizard or add expandable help with links to Puppet docs.

findings.title: Inconsistent product addon setup expectations
findings.description: Addons like CD4PE, SCM, and Operational Dashboard have different auto-setup levels. Help text is inconsistent about what's auto-installed vs manual.
findings.fix_suggestion: Standardize help text clearly stating install status (Auto-Installed, VM Only, Configuration Only). Add badges next to addon labels.

findings.title: No automated tests for frontend JavaScript
findings.description: Frontend has 1227 lines of JavaScript but no unit or integration tests.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add Jest/Vitest tests for form validation, API calls, detail rendering. Target 70%+ coverage.

findings.title: Duplicate configuration between frontend and backend
findings.description: Regions, OS options, machine types, PE versions, and costs are defined in both frontend and backend. Changes must be made twice.
findings.fix_suggestion: Fetch all config from backend /api/config. Remove hardcoded values from frontend.

findings.title: Hardcoded production API URL in config.js
findings.description: API endpoint is hardcoded pointing to production Cloud Run service. Local development requires manual changes.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use environment variable substitution at build time with fallback to relative '/api'.

findings.title: CORS enabled globally without authentication
findings.description: Flask CORS(app) with no restrictions allows any website to call the API. Combined with passphrase auth, creates CSRF vulnerability.
findings.fix_suggestion: Restrict CORS to known origins. Add CSRF token validation if using passphrase auth.

findings.title: No request validation schemas or type hints
findings.description: API uses manual validation with if/else chains. No Pydantic models. Functions lack type hints.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use Pydantic models for validation and type hints.

findings.title: No rate limiting on API endpoints
findings.description: API has no rate limiting. Users can spam requests without throttling.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add Flask-Limiter with rate limits per endpoint.

findings.title: No structured logging or observability
findings.description: All logging goes to Cloud Build logs. No structured application logs, metrics, or tracing.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add Python logging with JSON formatter. Send to Cloud Logging.

findings.title: Minimal test coverage for API
findings.description: Only test_api_validation.py exists. No tests for Cloud Build, metadata storage, or E2E deployment flow.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add integration tests for deployment lifecycle.

findings.title: No CI/CD pipeline for automated testing
findings.description: No GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or similar. Changes are manual; no automated tests before merge.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add GitHub Actions: run tests on PR, deploy on merge to main.

findings.title: No deployment comparison or diff view
findings.description: Cannot compare two deployments' configurations side-by-side.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add multi-select mode with side-by-side comparison of configurations.

findings.title: No deployment configuration export
findings.description: Cannot export deployment config as tfvars or JSON for reproducibility.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add endpoint to export original parameters. Add 'Export Config' button on detail view.

findings.title: Deployment name generation could be more meaningful
findings.description: Auto-generated names use random suffixes (e.g., 'pe-demo-3f7a8b9'). Hard to distinguish 10 deployments all starting with 'pe-demo'.
findings.fix_suggestion: Include requestor name and date: 'pe-demo-jane-20250322'.

findings.title: No mobile-responsive design
findings.description: Forms and tables overflow on mobile. Buttons are hard to tap.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add mobile breakpoints. Stack forms vertically on small screens. Increase button padding.

findings.title: No dark mode support
findings.description: UI is light-mode only. Modern users expect dark mode option.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add dark mode CSS variables and toggle.

findings.title: Missing JSDoc comments on JavaScript functions
findings.description: Functions lack JSDoc comments explaining parameters and return types.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add JSDoc to all public functions in app.js.

findings.title: No OpenAPI/Swagger specification
findings.description: API lacks machine-readable documentation. No OpenAPI spec or Swagger UI.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add Flask-RESTX to auto-generate OpenAPI docs.

findings.title: No webhook/notification integration
findings.description: No way to receive notifications (Slack, email, webhooks) when deployment completes.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add optional webhook_url in form. POST to webhook on status changes.

findings.title: Opportunity: Pre-built deployment templates
findings.description: Save common demo scenarios as templates (e.g., 'Standard + Agents', 'Large + CD4PE'). Currently users create from scratch each time.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create template library with 3-5 pre-built configs. Allow admins to add custom templates.

findings.title: Opportunity: Usage analytics and recommendations
findings.description: Track which architectures and products are used. Recommend optimizations and popular patterns.
findings.fix_suggestion: Aggregate metadata to track choices. Generate monthly usage reports.

findings.title: Opportunity: Cost optimization recommendations
findings.description: After deployment, analyze actual usage and suggest cheaper machine types if underutilized.
findings.fix_suggestion: Query agent count after deployment. If below threshold, recommend smaller architecture.

findings.title: Comprehensive README but scattered documentation
findings.description: 52KB README covers everything but is hard to navigate. Separate docs/ folder would help.
findings.fix_suggestion: Reorganize: docs/getting-started.md, docs/web-ui-guide.md, docs/troubleshooting.md. Link from README.

findings.title: No inline form help or field tooltips
findings.description: Form labels are brief. Help text is below fields but easy to miss. No tooltips on hover.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add help icons with hover tooltips for each field.

findings.title: No internationalization support
findings.description: UI is English-only. No i18n framework.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add i18n library if expanding globally. Translate to other languages.

findings.title: Event Publishing Flow Incomplete
findings.description: Marketing promises 'publish when ready' but the event publication flow from organizer preview to live production is incomplete. Coordinators cannot actually launch events to production—the flow stops at the build/preview stage. No publish endpoint, state transition logic, or live event population exists.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Add 'Publish Event' button to create/page.tsx after build completes. 2) Implement POST /api/events/{slug}/publish endpoint that: transitions event state from 'draft' to 'live', populates live event with builder data, sends guest notifications. 3) Add event status field to DynamoDB schema. 4) Create post-publish confirmation and success page.

findings.title: Real-Time Features Missing Despite Marketing Claims
findings.description: Product marketing emphasizes real-time engagement and live updates, but no real-time sync infrastructure exists. No WebSocket implementation, no sync status indicators in coordinator views, no offline handling beyond basic localStorage. Live leaderboard, activity feeds, and photo gallery are all static.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Implement WebSocket layer using API Gateway WebSocket API or Socket.io. 2) Add sync status indicator (Connected/Reconnecting/Offline) to event manage pages. 3) Subscribe coordinator dashboard to real-time updates: attendee check-ins, booth scans, new photos, activity feed. 4) Add offline queue for operations and sync on reconnect. 5) Test with concurrent edits to validate conflict resolution.

findings.title: Push Notifications Stubbed (Return 501)
findings.description: Push notification infrastructure referenced in settings and marketing but not wired. /api/push/send endpoint returns 200 with 'not configured' message and TODO comment about VAPID keys. Desktop/mobile notifications don't actually send.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Generate or retrieve VAPID keys from AWS Secrets Manager. 2) Wire up web-push library in Lambda. 3) Implement notification payload construction and delivery. 4) Add ServiceWorker push event handler. 5) Create notification preferences persistence. 6) Test end-to-end with demo event.

findings.title: Silent Failures on Critical Operations
findings.description: Delete, invite, and save operations fail silently with no user feedback. Error catch blocks swallow exceptions without showing confirmation or retry options. Users cannot tell if operations succeeded.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Add error boundary wrapper to all manage pages. 2) Implement consistent error toast notification: Toast.error(error.message, { actionLabel: 'Retry', onAction: retry }). 3) Catch all Promise rejections and display to user. 4) Provide 'Retry' button on all errors. 5) Log errors to backend for debugging. 6) Test with network offline scenario.

findings.title: No Confirmation Dialogs for Destructive Operations
findings.description: Delete operations (booths, guests, photos) execute immediately without user confirmation. Single accidental click removes data permanently with no recovery option.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Create ConfirmDeleteModal component with 'Cancel' and 'Delete' buttons. 2) Show what will be deleted. 3) Add 5-second undo window after successful delete. 4) Implement soft-delete in backend (mark deleted_at timestamp). 5) Add restore endpoint. 6) Show undo toast: Toast.success('Deleted. ', { actionLabel: 'Undo', onAction: restore }).

findings.title: Missing Loading States on Async Operations
findings.description: Booth creation, guest invitation, email sending, and photo approvals show no loading indicator. Users cannot tell if an operation is in progress and may click multiple times.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Add loading state to all forms: const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false). 2) Disable submit button while loading: disabled={isLoading}. 3) Show spinner: {isLoading && <Loader2 className='animate-spin' />}. 4) Prevent duplicate submissions. 5) Show progress toast during long operations. 6) Test with slow network via DevTools.

findings.title: Email Scheduling Not Functional
findings.description: Email scheduling UI exists in manage pages but real scheduling is not implemented. Email send goes only to demo mode; real event emails don't get scheduled or delivered.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Implement POST /api/events/{slug}/emails endpoint accepting: { to, subject, body, scheduleTime, recurrence }. 2) Store in DynamoDB with status 'scheduled'. 3) Create Lambda scheduled rule (EventBridge) to send queued emails at scheduled time. 4) Implement email delivery via SES. 5) Add delivery confirmation and bounce handling. 6) Show email history with delivery status.

findings.title: Gallery Provider Integration Not Implemented
findings.description: Gallery feature shows 'Not Set' and is disabled in demo. References to 'Analogify' and 'ChromaHaus processing' in code but no actual photo provider integration. Coordinators cannot add event photos.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Choose photo provider: AWS S3 + CloudFront, or third-party API (Flickr, Unsplash, etc). 2) Implement POST /api/events/{slug}/gallery/upload for S3 signed URLs. 3) Add photo list endpoint GET /api/events/{slug}/gallery/photos. 4) Implement coordinator approval workflow: POST /api/events/{slug}/gallery/approve/{photoId}. 5) Add photo gallery viewing to attendee event pages. 6) Add image optimization (resize, compress).

findings.title: 20+ Critical API Endpoints Missing
findings.description: Many backend endpoints required for feature completeness are missing or stubbed. Event updates, booth updates, member patches, analytics, gallery operations, bulk imports all missing.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Add event update endpoint: PUT /api/events/{slug} for name, tagline, location, dates. 2) Add booth patch: PATCH /api/events/{slug}/booths/{id} for description, image. 3) Add bulk operations: POST /api/events/{slug}/members/bulk-invite with CSV. 4) Add analytics endpoints: GET /api/events/{slug}/analytics/attendees, /booths, /sessions. 5) Implement all CRUD operations with proper auth. 6) Add OpenAPI schema.

findings.title: 325+ Hardcoded Demo Data Points Scattered Throughout Codebase
findings.description: Demo datasets (DEMO_MEMBERS, DEMO_BOOTHS, DEMO_ACTIVITY, DEMO_EMAIL_HISTORY, etc.) duplicated across 15+ manage pages. Making real API integration difficult and inconsistent.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Create centralized src/data/demo-factory.ts exporting all demo data. 2) Create one place to toggle demo vs real mode (already done in EventProvider). 3) Replace all inline DEMO_* with imports from factory. 4) Update hook to handle demo in one place: useEventData(apiPath, fallbackDemoData). 5) Add demo mode toggle in DemoExperienceBar to easily switch between demo/real on same page for testing.

findings.title: Test Coverage Gaps in Critical User Flows (74.6% statements, 61.7% branches)
findings.description: 25% of code paths are untested. Critical user flows lack coverage: event creation/building, invitation, check-in, booth scanning, email scheduling, photo approval.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Target 85%+ statement coverage, 75%+ branch coverage for src/app. 2) Add integration tests for coordinator workflows: event creation, guest invitation, check-in, booth scan. 3) Add tests for email scheduling end-to-end. 4) Test error handling: network failures, validation errors, auth failures. 5) Use test coverage reports to identify low-coverage files. 6) Add E2E tests with Playwright for critical flows.

findings.title: Empty State Messages Missing Guidance
findings.description: When lists are empty (no booths, no guests, no activity, no photos), pages show 'Loading...' or generic messages without explanation of how to populate the list.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Create EmptyState component showing: icon, title, description, call-to-action button. 2) Use in all list views: if (members.length === 0 && !loading) { return <EmptyState icon={Users} title='No guests yet' action='Invite guests' /> }. 3) Link CTA to relevant form. 4) Show step number if part of guided flow. 5) Update all manage page list views.

findings.title: Form Validation Feedback Missing
findings.description: Input fields lack validation feedback. Email field accepts invalid emails, booth names allow unlimited input, CSV fields accept malformed data without error messages.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Add real-time validation: handleChange triggers validate(). 2) Show inline error message on blur or submit. 3) Email validation: const isValidEmail = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(email). 4) Booth name: min 2 chars, max 100 chars, trim whitespace. 5) CSV validation: parse, validate each row, show errors. 6) Disable submit if validation fails. 7) Show character count on text fields.

findings.title: Mobile Layout Issues in Coordinator Interfaces
findings.description: Manage pages (booths, guests, emails) use grid layouts that don't stack properly on mobile. Buttons, forms remain wide. QR codes too small on mobile. No swipe actions or mobile affordances.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Refactor manage page layouts to mobile-first: stack vertically by default, grid on sm: breakpoint. 2) Booth QR: Add full-screen QR button, downloadable image. 3) Tables: add horizontal scroll with indicator, collapse columns on mobile. 4) Ensure tap targets >= 44px. 5) Add swipe-to-delete on list items. 6) Test on iPhone 12, Android phones.

findings.title: No Accessibility (ARIA Labels, Screen Reader Support)
findings.description: Coordinator manage pages lack screen reader support. No aria-labels on icon buttons, no aria-live regions for status updates, no role attributes on custom components.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Add aria-label to all icon buttons: <button aria-label='Delete booth'><X /></button>. 2) Add aria-live='polite' to toast region. 3) Add role='region' aria-label='Booth list' to lists. 4) Add aria-expanded, aria-controls to collapsible sections. 5) Add aria-busy='true' during loading. 6) Test with NVDA/JAWS screen reader.

findings.title: Duplicate API Token Handling Across Components
findings.description: localStorage.getItem('portis_token') repeated in 15+ components. No centralized token management, token refresh logic, or logout broadcast across tabs.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Create useApiToken hook: const { token, refreshToken, logout } = useApiToken(). 2) Implement token refresh: if token close to expiry, refresh before API call. 3) Listen for storage change event to sync logout across tabs. 4) Use SameSite=Strict cookies instead of localStorage. 5) Update all components to use hook instead of direct localStorage access.

findings.title: Inconsistent Error Handling Patterns
findings.description: Some endpoints try/catch and show errors, others silently fail. No standardized error handling, no structured error logging to backend, inconsistent error message formats.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Create ErrorBoundary component that catches React errors. 2) Wrap all manage pages in <ErrorBoundary>. 3) Implement centralized error logger: logError(error, context). 4) Send errors to Sentry or CloudWatch. 5) Standardize error response format. 6) Show user-friendly error messages, log full error for debugging.

findings.title: No Typed API Client
findings.description: Fetch calls scattered throughout codebase with untyped responses. No type safety on API requests/responses. Every component does its own fetch with unique error handling.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Create src/lib/api-client.ts with typed fetch wrapper. 2) Define request/response types for each endpoint. 3) Use zod for runtime validation of responses. 4) Generate types from OpenAPI/GraphQL schema if available. 5) Replace all fetch calls with typed client: api.get('/events/:slug/members', token).

findings.title: Text-to-Speech Endpoint Returns 501
findings.description: TTS endpoint exists but returns 'AWS Polly integration pending' error. No voice synthesis capability despite API endpoint definition.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Integrate AWS Polly. 2) Accept text, voice, language params. 3) Cache results in S3. 4) Return presigned S3 URL. 5) Add cost controls (cache TTL, max chars). 6) Test with event session descriptions.

findings.title: No Event Data Export (Analytics, Attendance, Leads)
findings.description: No way for coordinators to export event data. Marketing claims 'analytics' but no export to CSV/PDF. No attendance reports, lead capture reports, session popularity reports.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Create /api/events/{slug}/export?format=csv endpoint. 2) Export attendance: members, check-in times, roles, badges. 3) Export analytics: session popularity, booth scans, photo views, leaderboard. 4) Export leads: booth visitors, contact captures, follow-ups. 5) Add async job system for large exports. 6) Add export download buttons to manage pages.

findings.title: No Bulk Operations (CSV Import, Batch Invite, Batch Actions)
findings.description: Coordinators must add guests/booths one at a time. No CSV import, no batch invite, no bulk role assignment.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Add CSV upload to guest page: accepts name, email, role. 2) Parse and validate before insert. 3) Show preview of imported data. 4) Create /api/events/{slug}/members/bulk-import endpoint. 5) Handle duplicates (skip, merge, update). 6) Send bulk invite emails via SES. 7) Same for booths: CSV template download, bulk import.

findings.title: No Automation Workflows (Scheduled Actions, Triggers)
findings.description: No automation beyond static reminders. Missing: auto-generated reminder emails 1 day/1 hour before, auto-assign roles by email domain, trigger-based notifications (booth visit, check-in).
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Create workflow builder UI (coordinator configurable). 2) Templates: reminder email 1d before, 1h before, post-event survey. 3) Triggers: send email when guest checks in, when booth is visited. 4) Use EventBridge rules for scheduled jobs, SNS for event-triggered. 5) Test with demo event workflows.

findings.title: No Search/Filter in Manage Lists
findings.description: Guest list shows all members, no search by name/email. Booth list unsearchable. No filter by role, check-in status, or date range. No saved filters.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Add search input above list: onChange triggers filter. 2) Add filter buttons: role, check-in status, date joined. 3) Implement client-side filtering for now, server-side if list grows large. 4) Save frequently-used filters. 5) Same for booth, email, activity lists.

findings.title: Incomplete Data Models (Missing Fields)
findings.description: User, Event, Member types missing critical fields: createdAt, updatedAt, publishedAt, archivedAt, lastModifiedBy, lastActiveAt, notificationPreferences.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Update User type: add createdAt, updatedAt, preferences (notifications, theme), subscription info. 2) Create Event type with full schema. 3) Update Member type: add permissions array, lastActiveAt, notificationPreferences, badges array. 4) Update DynamoDB schema with new fields (backfill nulls). 5) Create migrations docs.

findings.title: No Soft Deletes (Deletions Are Permanent)
findings.description: All delete operations are hard deletions. No recovery window, no trash/restore feature, no audit trail of deletions.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Implement soft-delete: add deleted_at timestamp instead of removing. 2) Exclude soft-deleted items from list queries. 3) Add restore endpoint. 4) Purge hard-deleted items after 30-day retention. 5) Log deletion (who, when) for audit trail. 6) Add trash/restore UI in coordinator views.

findings.title: No API Documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger)
findings.description: No OpenAPI spec, no API endpoint documentation, no request/response examples, no error code reference, no rate limits documented.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Create OpenAPI 3.0 spec file: openapi.yaml. 2) Document all endpoints: path, method, params, request body, response, error codes. 3) Generate interactive docs with Swagger UI or ReDoc. 4) Add to GitHub Pages or dev docs site. 5) Keep spec in sync with implementation.

findings.title: No Centralized Error Logging
findings.description: Errors logged to console only or not at all. No backend error collection, no error rate monitoring, cannot debug production issues.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Integrate Sentry or Datadog. 2) Wrap app in <Sentry.ErrorBoundary>. 3) Capture all unhandled rejections. 4) Send errors with context: user, event, operation. 5) Set up alerts for error spikes. 6) Create error dashboard.

findings.title: Hardcoded Admin Email
findings.description: Admin access hardcoded to 'cody@codyjo.com'. No way to add admins without code change. No admin user management interface.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Move admin emails to environment variable or database table. 2) Create admin user management page (create, revoke, view). 3) Implement role-based access control (RBAC) in API. 4) Store admin status in User table. 5) Audit admin actions.

findings.title: Email Verification Incomplete
findings.description: Settings page shows email verification state but enforcement missing. No verification requirement for signup or event publication.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Send verification email on signup. 2) Require verification before event publication. 3) Show unverified banner. 4) Resend verification link. 5) Auto-verify known email domains (gmail, outlook, etc) if appropriate.

findings.title: Dependency Updates Available
findings.description: Several dependencies have minor/patch updates available. @anthropic-ai/sdk 0.78→0.80, AWS SDK 3.1011→3.1014, Next.js 16.1.7→16.2.1, others.
