findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Run npm update to apply patch/minor updates. 2) Test thoroughly (run test suite). 3) Audit for breaking changes in major versions. 4) Plan Next.js 17+ upgrade for future. 5) Set up Dependabot to automate this.

findings.title: No Webhooks for External Integration
findings.description: No way for external systems to react to event changes. Missing: attendee joined, booth visited, photo approved, email sent events.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Design webhook schema (event type, payload, retry logic). 2) Implement webhook registration endpoint. 3) Store webhooks in DynamoDB. 4) Emit webhook on key events (attendee joined, booth scanned, photo approved). 5) Use SQS or SNS for reliable delivery. 6) Add webhook test/replay UI in coordinator dashboard.

findings.title: No Undo/Redo Functionality
findings.description: Users cannot undo accidental operations. No operation history, no undo API endpoint, no time-travel UI.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Implement operation command pattern. 2) Maintain operation history stack. 3) Add undo/redo buttons or Ctrl+Z support. 4) Persist history to backend for cross-session undo. 5) Add operation history sidebar showing recent changes.

findings.title: No Speaker/Vendor Self-Service Portal
findings.description: Speakers and vendors cannot edit their own information. Coordinators must manage all content. Missing: self-service profile update, photo upload, bio editing, session description.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Create /e/{slug}/speaker/{speakerId}/settings page. 2) Allow edit: bio, photo, social links, session description. 3) Same for /vendor/{vendorId}/settings. 4) Add save/draft functionality. 5) Require coordinator approval for changes or auto-publish. 6) Add notification when profile is edited.

findings.title: No Post-Event Surveys or Feedback Collection
findings.description: No feedback collection from attendees. Missing: satisfaction surveys, NPS, photo rating, session feedback.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Create survey builder UI (coordinator can create custom surveys). 2) Deploy surveys post-event or via email. 3) Collect responses with timestamp. 4) Aggregate results: charts, ratings, open-ended responses. 5) Export survey results. 6) Correlate with attendance data (who attended which sessions, rated which).

findings.title: Leaderboard & Gamification Not Configurable
findings.description: Leaderboard and badge system are seeded demo-only. Coordinators cannot configure rules, point multipliers, or badge criteria.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Create gamification settings in coordinator dashboard. 2) UI for: point values per action (booth scan, photo, check-in), badge rules, leaderboard visibility. 3) Backend: Store rules in DynamoDB, compute leaderboard based on rules. 4) Real-time leaderboard updates. 5) Per-event configuration (different rules for different events).

findings.title: No Multi-Language Support (i18n)
findings.description: Site is English-only. No i18n infrastructure for international events.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Add i18n library (next-intl or i18next). 2) Extract all copy to translation files. 3) Support: English, Spanish, French, German, Mandarin (start). 4) Coordinator can set event language. 5) Attendee sees site in event language or their preference.

findings.title: No Mobile App (Native or PWA)
findings.description: Marketing mentions 'no app download' but web app is not PWA-enabled for offline use, home screen install, or notifications.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Enhance manifest.json with full config. 2) Implement Service Worker offline caching. 3) Cache critical routes offline. 4) Sync data when online. 5) Add 'Add to Home Screen' banner. 6) Test on mobile browsers.

findings.title: No Analytics Beyond Plausible
findings.description: Only Plausible.io analytics configured. Missing: funnel analysis, cohort analysis, custom events, retention metrics.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Track custom events: event_created, booth_scanned, member_invited, photo_approved. 2) Create funnels: signup → event creation → publish. 3) Set up cohort analysis. 4) Create dashboards for KPIs. 5) Use data to prioritize roadmap.

findings.title: No Social Features (Sharing, Comments, Networking)
findings.description: No attendee social features. Missing: ability to add attendees as contacts, comment on sessions, share photos to social.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Add 'Add as Contact' button to profiles. 2) Create attendee contact list in journal. 3) Allow comments on sessions, photos. 4) Social sharing buttons for photos/sessions. 5) @mention attendees in comments. 6) Create private chat for attendees (if appropriate).

findings.title: No Rate Limiting Documentation
findings.description: API has rate limiting but no public documentation of limits, no rate limit headers in responses.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Document rate limits in API docs (per endpoint). 2) Add X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset headers to responses. 3) Return 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header when limit exceeded. 4) Create rate limit FAQ. 5) Provide rate limit test endpoint.

findings.title: No Referral/Affiliate Program
findings.description: No way for users to share and earn referral credits. Missing: referral links, tracking, reward program.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Generate unique referral links for each user. 2) Track referrals: who referred whom. 3) Reward: account credit, feature unlock, or commission. 4) Create referral dashboard: link, stats, earnings. 5) Marketing: referral landing page with incentive. 6) Track to Plausible for analysis.

findings.title: No White-Label/Reseller Support
findings.description: No way for agencies or resellers to offer Cordivent under their brand. Missing: custom domain support, white-labeled emails, branding settings.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Add custom domain support (CNAME for event URL). 2) Create branding settings: logo, colors, fonts, email template. 3) Hide Cordivent branding if reseller customizes. 4) Reseller account type with subdomain support. 5) Pricing tier for white-label. 6) Test with custom domain.

findings.title: Awaiting Reflection filter incomplete in journal
findings.description: The journal list UI shows an 'Awaiting Reflection' filter option (key: 'unreflected'), but the backend API does not support filtering by reflection status. The filter UI renders but has no effect, creating a broken UX where users think they can filter but the feature doesn't work.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Add 'awaitingReflection' boolean field to journal schema. 2) Implement toggle in journal entry UI. 3) Add filtering support in getJournalEntries API. 4) Filter UI should only show when entries exist with this status. 5) Add test coverage for filter behavior.

findings.title: Push notifications not implemented despite UI preference toggles
findings.description: Settings page includes three practice rhythm preference toggles (daily reminders, weekly reviews, milestone celebrations) with localStorage persistence, but these preferences are never actually used to send notifications. No notification infrastructure exists.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Implement Service Worker for Web Push API. 2) Add subscription endpoints in Lambda API. 3) Create backend notification scheduler (EventBridge + Lambda). 4) Send notifications based on stored preferences. 5) Add notification permission prompt on first interaction. 6) Provide notification history/log. Start with email notifications (simpler, requires Resend integration).

findings.title: Favorites with notes incomplete — ported from bible-app but missing note storage
findings.description: Reading history supports toggling favorites via toggleFavorite() API and shows favorite hearts in UI, but there is no backend support for favorite notes. The note editing UI exists (editingNoteId state in history/page.tsx) but saveFavoriteNote() likely fails silently.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Add 'notes' string field to READING item schema. 2) Implement saveFavoriteNote endpoint in Lambda API. 3) Load notes when fetching readings. 4) Update history UI to call saveFavoriteNote on text change (with debounce). 5) Display notes in reading detail view. 6) Add tests.
